title: lasthenia macrantha var. bakeri
intro: Lasthenia macrantha var. bakeri, also known as the Bakers' Lasthenia or yellow daisy, is a rare and endangered annual plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It is native to California, USA, and is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Lasthenia macrantha var. bakeri can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used in folk medicine to promote healing and well-being.
